verses.text: Asaph prays to the 'Shepherd of Israel' to shine forth and stir up His strength, with the refrain, 'Turn us again, O God... and we shall be saved.' Restoration must begin with God. We are saved only when He turns us back to Himself.

Asaph prays to the 'Shepherd of Israel' to shine forth and stir up His strength, with the refrain, 'Turn us again, O God... and we shall be saved.' Restoration must begin with God. We are saved only when He turns us back to Himself.

Asaph prays to the 'Shepherd of Israel' to shine forth and stir up His strength, with the refrain, 'Turn us again, O God... and we shall be saved.' Restoration must begin with God. We are saved only when He turns us back to Himself.

'How long' will God be angry at His people's prayer, feeding them with tears? Again he pleads, 'Turn us again, O God of hosts.' The refrain rises with urgency. Repeated prayer presses God for the renewing only He can give.

'How long' will God be angry at His people's prayer, feeding them with tears? Again he pleads, 'Turn us again, O God of hosts.' The refrain rises with urgency. Repeated prayer presses God for the renewing only He can give.

'How long' will God be angry at His people's prayer, feeding them with tears? Again he pleads, 'Turn us again, O God of hosts.' The refrain rises with urgency. Repeated prayer presses God for the renewing only He can give.
He recalls how God brought 'a vine out of Egypt,' planted it, and made it fill the land and spread to the sea. Israel was His own planting. God had tended His people with loving care.
He recalls how God brought 'a vine out of Egypt,' planted it, and made it fill the land and spread to the sea. Israel was His own planting. God had tended His people with loving care.
He recalls how God brought 'a vine out of Egypt,' planted it, and made it fill the land and spread to the sea. Israel was His own planting. God had tended His people with loving care.
He recalls how God brought 'a vine out of Egypt,' planted it, and made it fill the land and spread to the sea. Israel was His own planting. God had tended His people with loving care.
Why then has God broken down its hedges so that passers-by pluck it and the wild boar wastes it? The vineyard lies exposed. When God withdraws His protection, His people are laid open to ruin.
Why then has God broken down its hedges so that passers-by pluck it and the wild boar wastes it? The vineyard lies exposed. When God withdraws His protection, His people are laid open to ruin.
'Return, we beseech thee, O God of hosts... and visit this vine,' burned and cut down before His rebuke. He begs God to come back. The only hope of the wasted vineyard is the Keeper's return.
'Return, we beseech thee, O God of hosts... and visit this vine,' burned and cut down before His rebuke. He begs God to come back. The only hope of the wasted vineyard is the Keeper's return.
'Return, we beseech thee, O God of hosts... and visit this vine,' burned and cut down before His rebuke. He begs God to come back. The only hope of the wasted vineyard is the Keeper's return.

He prays God's hand be on 'the man of thy right hand,' and pledges they will not turn back; once more, 'Turn us again, O LORD... cause thy face to shine.' The threefold refrain closes the psalm. Salvation is God turning to us and us to Him.
